sections.title: Core Functional Job (L7)
sections.content:
**CFJ Statement:** Manufacture climate control and thermal management equipment that conditions, heats, cools, or refrigerates enclosed spaces and stored goods to specified temperature and humidity setpoints.

*Industry-level — no reference to any specific technology, product, or company. Covers all output types: residential AC, heat pumps, warm air furnaces, commercial refrigeration, industrial chillers.*

sections.title: Output Types
sections.content:
| ID | Output Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| OT-1 | Residential Split/Packaged AC | Window units, split systems, mini-splits, packaged rooftop residential |
| OT-2 | Heat Pump (air-source / water-source) | Reversible vapor-compression units for heating + cooling |
| OT-3 | Warm Air Furnace | Gas-fired, oil-fired, or electric forced-air furnaces |
| OT-4 | Commercial Rooftop Unit (RTU) | Packaged HVAC for commercial buildings |
| OT-5 | Chiller (air-cooled / water-cooled) | Central cooling plant equipment for large commercial/industrial |
| OT-6 | Commercial Refrigeration Equipment | Display cases, walk-in coolers/freezers, reach-in units |
| OT-7 | Industrial Refrigeration Equipment | Ammonia systems, blast freezers, cold storage |

---

sections.content:

sections.title: L6 — Core Process Steps (Sequential)
sections.content:
| L6 | Name | Job Family | Output Types |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | **Sheet Metal Fabrication** | Cut, form, punch, and bend sheet metal into enclosure and structural components | OT-1 through OT-7 (all) |
| L6b | **Heat Exchanger Fabrication** | Manufacture coils, condensers, and evaporator assemblies by bending tubing and attaching fins | OT-1, OT-2, OT-4, OT-5, OT-6, OT-7 |
| L6c | **Refrigerant Circuit Assembly** | Braze/weld tubing connections, mount compressor, expansion device, and reversing valve into integrated refrigerant circuit | OT-1, OT-2, OT-4, OT-5, OT-6, OT-7 |
| L6d | **Combustion Assembly** | Assemble burner, heat exchanger, gas valve, ignition, and flue components into heating module | OT-3 (furnace only) |
| L6e | **Electrical & Controls Integration** | Wire motors, contactors, sensors, control boards, and communication interfaces into the unit | OT-1 through OT-7 (all) |
| L6f | **Hydronic Circuit Assembly** | Assemble water-side piping, circulation pumps, flow sensors, and hydraulic components for water-based heat exchange | OT-2 (water-source HP), OT-5 (water-cooled chiller) |
| L6g | **System Evacuation & Refrigerant Charging** | Evacuate system to vacuum, leak-test, charge with specified refrigerant mass | OT-1, OT-2, OT-4, OT-5, OT-6, OT-7 |
| L6h | **Final Assembly & Enclosure** | Mount all sub-assemblies into cabinet, attach panels, insulation, labels, and shipping hardware | OT-1 through OT-7 (all) |
| L6i | **Performance Testing & Certification** | Run functional test, verify capacity/efficiency against AHRI/UL/CE standards, pass or reject | OT-1 through OT-7 (all) |

sections.title: L6 — Horizontal Process Steps
sections.content:
| L6 | Name | Job Family | Scope |

| L6(H2) | **Maintenance & Engineering** | Maintain production equipment uptime, tooling calibration, and process engineering | All core steps |
| L6(H3) | **HSE & Environmental Compliance** | Manage refrigerant handling (EPA Section 608), brazing fume extraction, noise, and worker safety | All core steps |
| L6(H4) | **Utilities & Facility Management** | Supply compressed air, nitrogen, electrical power, HVAC, and water to production floor | All core steps |
| L6(H5) | **IT / Operations Technology** | Manage MES, ERP, CNC program distribution, test data acquisition, and traceability | All core steps |

```
L6b Heat Exchanger Fab    x     x           x     x     x     x

L6d Combustion Assembly                x
L6e Electrical & Ctrl     x     x     x     x     x     x     x
L6f Hydronic Circuit            x                 x
L6g Evacuation & Charge   x     x           x     x     x     x
L6h Final Assembly        x     x     x     x     x     x     x
L6i Performance Test      x     x     x     x     x     x     x
```

**Key insight:** L6d (Combustion Assembly) is activated only by OT-3 (furnaces) — highly specialized. L6f (Hydronic Circuit) is activated only by water-source heat pumps and water-cooled chillers — where the Marquardt flow sensor plays. L6a, L6e, L6h, L6i are universal infrastructure steps.

sections.title: Marquardt Sensor Position in VN
sections.content:
The Marquardt Ultrasonic Flow Sensor occupies the **Flow Sensor (L5)** position within **L6f: Hydronic Circuit Assembly**. This L6 node is activated only by OT-2 (water-source heat pumps) and OT-5 (water-cooled chillers) — the sensor's current home market.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **VN Anchor Level** | L5 |
| **VN Anchor Node** | Flow Sensor |
| **Parent L6** | L6f — Hydronic Circuit Assembly |
| **Sibling L5s** | Circulation Pump, Plate Heat Exchanger, Hydraulic Valve Assembly |
| **L4 Children** | Transducer Assembly, Signal Conditioning Electronics, Flow Body / Housing |
| **Alternative Technologies** | Ultrasonic transit-time (35%), Turbine/impeller (30%), Vortex (15%), Differential pressure (10%), Paddle/float switch (10%) |
| **Cost of Flow Sensor Failure** | System cannot regulate thermal output -> compressor short-cycling, freeze protection loss, efficiency degradation, potential equipment damage |
